description_long: This object consists of a large, cylindrical wooden base supporting a heavy, black metal anvil on top. The wooden base is robust and appears to be made from a solid log, with a dark, weathered finish and subtle grain patterns. The anvil is a classic London-pattern design, featuring a flat top surface, a pointed horn on one end, and a square hardy hole on the opposite end. The anvil is securely mounted to the wooden base, with visible metal brackets or supports at the base for stability. The overall appearance suggests durability and industrial use, with the metal showing a matte, slightly rough texture and the wood displaying signs of age and use. There are no visible handles, lids, or markings on the exterior surfaces.
description: A heavy black metal anvil mounted on a large, dark wooden cylindrical base, with visible metal supports and a weathered, industrial appearance.
description_view_0: The pointed horn of the anvil is centered, with the cylindrical wooden base clearly visible and the metal supports flanking the anvil.
description_view_1: The flat working face and hardy hole of the anvil are prominent, showing the full length of the anvil atop the wooden base.
description_view_2: The rear of the anvil with the hardy hole is centered, and the wooden base is fully visible beneath.
description_view_3: The opposite side of the anvil is shown, with the horn extending to the left and the flat face to the right, all atop the wooden base.
